title: Netflix explains how it serves large language models in-house
friendlyTitle: Netflix shares how it runs AI models on its own systems
summary: Netflix’s AI platform teams describe an internal LLM serving stack built around vLLM, NVIDIA Triton and a shared production control plane. They expose an OpenAI-compatible HTTP interface alongside existing gRPC services, while documenting version mismatches, custom-model escape hatches and rollout trade-offs discovered under load.
friendlySummary: Netflix built an internal service for running large language models. It uses common open-source tools and a standard API so teams can move between hosted and self-run models more easily.
whyItMatters: The account offers a grounded blueprint for organisations considering self-hosted models for privacy, cost or customisation. Standard APIs can make model swaps easier, but production reliability still depends on strict version pinning, observability and controlled deployment paths.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can help engineering teams plan private AI systems. The important work is not only choosing a model, but also testing versions, watching failures and updating safely.
